query: I need to use @FetchAll in my view model, which skill should I use?
expected_behavior: Routes to sqlitedata-swift-core skill (not ref or diag)
Identifies @FetchAll as a core pattern keyword
Does not attempt to answer the question directly without routing

query: My SyncEngine is throwing an error about UNIQUE constraints
expected_behavior: Routes to sqlitedata-swift-diag skill (troubleshooting keyword: error, throwing)
Does not route to cloudkit skill despite SyncEngine mention
Prioritizes error/troubleshooting routing over feature routing

query: What is the full API for SyncEngine sharing methods?
expected_behavior: Launches the sqlitedata-reference agent (API signatures are handled by the agent)
Does not route to sqlitedata-swift-core or sqlitedata-swift-diag
Identifies 'API' and 'SyncEngine' as agent-routed keywords

query: How do I define a @Table model with a custom column coding?
expected_behavior: Shows @Table struct with nonisolated keyword
Demonstrates @Column(as:) for custom coding
Mentions let id: UUID as the primary key pattern
Does not omit nonisolated (critical gotcha #2)

query: How do I set up the database and run migrations?
expected_behavior: Shows prepareDependencies call at app entry point
Shows DatabaseMigrator with #sql macro for CREATE TABLE
Includes STRICT keyword on table definition
Includes NOT NULL ON CONFLICT REPLACE DEFAULT pattern
Shows configuration.foreignKeysEnabled = true

query: How do I use @FetchAll with a join query in an @Observable model?
expected_behavior: Shows @FetchAll with leftJoin and .select clause
Includes @ObservationIgnored annotation (critical gotcha #1)
Shows @MainActor @Observable class pattern
Does not omit @ObservationIgnored (most common mistake)

query: How do I set up SyncEngine with both shared and private tables?
expected_behavior: Shows SyncEngine init with both tables: and privateTables: parameters
Explains the distinction: tables are shareable, privateTables are not
Shows prepareDependencies setup with both defaultDatabase and defaultSyncEngine
Mentions containerIdentifier parameter

query: What schema constraints does CloudKit sync require?
expected_behavior: Lists UUID primary keys as required (not integer autoincrement)
Explains NOT NULL ON CONFLICT REPLACE DEFAULT pattern
States UNIQUE constraints are not allowed on synced tables
Lists allowed ON DELETE actions (CASCADE, SET NULL, SET DEFAULT)
Mentions reserved CloudKit field names to avoid

query: How do I handle account changes when the user signs out of iCloud?
expected_behavior: Shows SyncEngineDelegate implementation with accountChanged method
Shows alert pattern for signOut/switchAccounts cases
Mentions deleteLocalData() as the cleanup action
Notes default behavior auto-deletes if no delegate is set

query: How do I enable iCloud with CloudKit in Xcode?
expected_behavior: Describes adding iCloud capability in Xcode
Says to check CloudKit in the Services section
Mentions entitlements added automatically
Describes container creation with iCloud. prefix

query: How do I deploy my schema to production before submitting to the App Store?
expected_behavior: Directs to CloudKit Console at icloud.developer.apple.com
Describes Deploy Schema Changes flow
Warns that deploys are additive-only (cannot delete record types/fields)
Mentions admin privileges requirement

query: What background mode do I need for CloudKit push?
expected_behavior: Specifies Remote notifications background mode
Shows the Info.plist value: remote-notification
Explains this is needed for SyncEngine to receive server change notifications

query: What are all the initializers for @FetchAll?
expected_behavior: Lists all init variants: default table fetch, SelectStatement, Statement<V>
Shows animation and scheduler variants
Shows properties: wrappedValue, projectedValue, loadError, isLoading, publisher
Shows methods: load(), load(_:database:)

query: What is the SyncEngine.share method signature?
expected_behavior: Shows share<T: PrimaryKeyedTable>(record: T, configure: (CKShare) -> Void) async throws -> SharedRecord
Shows unshare and acceptShare signatures
References sharing-ref skill for CloudKit sharing model details

query: How do I use FTS5 full-text search with SQLiteData?
expected_behavior: Uses the inline FTS5 section in the ref skill
Shows @Table struct conforming to FTS5 protocol
Shows CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE SQL with fts5 and tokenize
Mentions keeping FTS in sync via triggers

query: My @FetchAll isn't updating when I write new data
expected_behavior: First checks: is @ObservationIgnored present on the fetch property wrapper?
Checks: is the database the same instance from prepareDependencies?
Checks: was prepareDependencies called exactly once?
Does not suggest unrelated fixes (migration issues, schema problems)

query: SyncEngine init throws about UNIQUE constraint
expected_behavior: Identifies: UNIQUE constraints on non-PK columns are not allowed with sync
Recommends removing UNIQUE constraint from synced table
Suggests making the unique column the primary key as workaround
Does not suggest adding ON CONFLICT REPLACE (that's for NOT NULL, not UNIQUE)

query: I get 'no such table' error when running my app
expected_behavior: Checks: migration registered and migrator.migrate(database) called
Checks: table name matches between @Table and SQL CREATE TABLE
Checks: prepareDependencies called (especially in previews/tests)
Does not blame CloudKit sync for a local schema issue

query: How does SQLiteData map UUIDs to CKRecord.ID?
expected_behavior: Explains UUID becomes the CKRecord.ID recordName
States record names must be ASCII, max 255 chars, unique within zone
Explains this is why SQLiteData requires UUID primary keys
Mentions CKRecordZone.ID as the other component of CKRecord.ID

query: What permissions can shared record participants have?
expected_behavior: Lists .readOnly, .readWrite, .none permissions
Mentions public share (anyone with URL)
Notes SQLiteData enforces write permission via SyncEngine.writePermissionError

query: How do I present UICloudSharingController?
expected_behavior: Shows controller creation for both new shares and existing shares
Shows delegate methods (didSaveShare, didStopSharing, failedToSave)
Shows availablePermissions configuration
Mentions CKSharingSupported Info.plist requirement

query: What does SwiftData do automatically that SQLiteData does explicitly?
expected_behavior: Explains SwiftData auto-discovers container from entitlements
Explains SwiftData auto-creates CloudKit schema (SQLiteData uses SyncEngine)
Notes both share the same underlying CloudKit constraints
Mentions schemas are additive-only in both

query: What SwiftData macros are not CloudKit-compatible?
expected_behavior: Lists @Attribute(.unique) — CloudKit cannot enforce unique constraints
Lists @Relationship — must be optional, .deny delete rule not supported
Draws parallel to SQLiteData's equivalent constraints
Does not confuse SwiftData macros with SQLiteData @Table syntax

query: How do I disable SwiftData's automatic CloudKit sync?
expected_behavior: Shows ModelConfiguration(cloudKitDatabase: .none)
Explains this is for apps that use CloudKit directly (like SQLiteData)
Does not suggest removing iCloud capability entirely
